entry.title: SAP Joule for Consultant (J4C) | SAP Business AI
entry.content:
Hello Community,

SAP Joule for Consultants accelerates SAP projects and cloud transformations with the most authoritative AI assistance grounded in exclusive SAP knowledge.

It enables consultants to increase productivity with fast and reliable answers, minimize rework with expert-level guidance that improves every design decision, and deliver more operational efficiency by ensuring projects align with best practices.

SAP Joule for Consultants provides comprehensive explanation of ABAP code purpose, business logic, and structure, powered by a fine-tuned model trained on the largest and most diverse repository of ABAP code, encompassing 250 million lines of ABAP and 30 million lines of CDS code.

Its expert-level guidance is grounded in 9+ terabytes of curated SAP knowledge, including 100+ SAP certifications and 3+ million non-public documents such as SAP Notes and SAP Knowledge Base Articles.

What is the Business value?

  • Up to 10.5% improved productivity through enhanced knowledge searches
  • 50% less design iterations and subsequent rework
  • 1.5% increase in annual profit growth enabled by SAP process optimization
  • 13.7% faster time to realized value

Need more information?

Check out our discovery center https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/ai-feature/3c3c6f7d-7310-47a9-ae52-d7ddd2f0c2f2/

#SAPJoule #Business AI

entry.title: Developing AI Enabled Applications with ABAP AI SDK powered by ISLM
entry.content:
SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI Capabilities is your intelligent coding companion, bringing deep expertise in ABAP Cloud and AI-driven development. When we talk about ABAP AI use cases, they revolve around three key pillars — Accelerate, Empower, and Transform. Each AI capability is delivered as part of these pillars, helping developers enhance productivity, build smarter applications, and drive innovation. To learn more about SAP Joule for Developers, refer to the blog post Joule speaks ABAP! by Dr. Alexander Rother.

Have you ever wondered how to infuse intelligence into your ABAP-based business applications and move beyond standard transactional scenarios? If yes, you’ve landed at the right place. In this blog, we’ll explore how you can build AI-enabled applications using the ABAP AI SDK powered by Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM), the main deliverable of the Empower use case.

The Scenario 
The goal of this scenario is to infuse generative AI into an ABAP Cloud application to enhance user experience and automate content creation. 

Use Case: Build an ABAP Cloud application to generate a travel itinerary for a given city based on user input, along with a budget breakdown, using ABAP AI SDK powered by ISLM. 

Which products are supported? 

The ABAP AI SDK powered by ISLM is available in: 

  • SAP BTP ABAP Environment
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA OnPrem as of release 2025 via standard delivery. Lower releases of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA OnPrem are supported via Transport-based Correction Instructions (TCI), see details in SAP Note 3513374 and the SAP Notes and instructions linked therein. 

Overview 

To build this application, we’ll bring together several key components: 

  • SAP AI Core – for managing AI scenarios and accessing generative AI capabilities via the generative AI hub. 
  • Communication Arrangement – to establish secure integration between ABAP and AI Core. 
  • ISLM (Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management) and ABAP AI SDK – for seamless integration with Generative AI Hub, SAP BTP AI services 
  • Custom ABAP Cloud Application – to embed and expose AI capabilities through a user-friendly application. 

 Implementation Steps 

  1. Set Up the Environment 
    Configure the AI Core service plan and establish communication arrangements between the ABAP system and AI Core.
  2. Build Custom Travel Plan ABAP Cloud application. 
  3. Create and Deploy Intelligent Scenarios 
    Define the AI scenario in ISLM, train or connect your model, and deploy it for consumption. 
  4. Consume Intelligent Scenarios in ABAP 
    Integrate the deployed scenario within a custom ABAP Cloud application to generate AI-driven outputs - in this case, travel itineraries and budget breakdown. 

1. Set Up the Environment  

  1. Configure the required entitlements to make SAP AI Core accessible in your subaccount. To use Generative AI capabilities (including the Generative AI Hub and LLM access), choose the extended service plan. To add a Service Plan, see: Add a Service Plan | SAP Help Portal  
  2. Download the client default certificate of the ABAP system to create the X.509 service key in AI Core instance. Follow the steps here : Download Certificate | SAP Help Portal  
  3. Create a service instance and a service key to access SAP AI Core, where the large language models in the generative AI hub are hosted. The X.509 service key is created with the downloaded client default certificate of the ABAP system passed as parameter. Follow the steps from here: Create SAP BTP Service Instance and Key | SAP Help Portal
  4. Configure the communication system for SAP_COM_0A69. Currently, you can create one instance of the communication scenario per client. Follow the steps from here: How to Configure the Communication System for SAP_COM_0A69 | SAP Help Portal 
  5. To create the Communication Arrangement for SAP_COM_0A69. Follow the steps from here: How to Create the Communication Arrangement for SAP_COM_0A69 | SAP Help Portal 
  6. After the communication system and communication arrangement are configured, create an intelligent scenario and continue managing the lifecycle of the intelligent scenario. You are required to create an intelligent scenario for your custom-owned Gen AI use cases. 

2. Create a custom ABAP Cloud Application to infuse AI Capabilities 

2.1 Create Package

  1. In ABAP Development Tools (ADT), go to the Project Explorer, right-click on the package ZLOCAL, and select New → ABAP Package from the context menu.
  2. Maintain the required information: 
    1. Name: ZTRAVEL_ITINERARY
    2. Description: ABAP AI Capabilities
      Select the box Add to favorites package. Choose Next and Finish.

 2.2 Create database table 

Create a database table to store the Travel Itinerary data.

  1. Right-click on your ABAP package ZTRAVEL_ITINERARY and select New → Other ABAP Repository Object from the context menu.
  2. Search for database table, select it, and choose Next.
  3. Maintain the required information: Name: ZRAP_TRAVEL_DATA   Description: Database Table for Travel Itinerary
  4. Choose Next. Choose Finish and replace the default code with the following code snippet: 
@EndUserText.label : 'Database table for travel Itinerary'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED
define table zrap_travel_data {
  key client            : abap.clnt not null;
  key travel_id         : sysuuid_x16 not null;
  traveler_name         : abap.char(80);
  starting_point        : abap.char(100);
  destination           : abap.char(100);
  start_date            : /dmo/begin_date;
  end_date              : /dmo/end_date;
  @Semantics.amount.currencyCode : 'zrap_travel_data.budget_curr'
  budget                : abap.curr(15,2);
  budget_curr           : abap.cuky;
  transport_mode        : abap.char(40);
  accommodation_type    : abap.char(50);
  ai_itinerary          : abap.sstring(1333);
  ai_budget_breakdown   : abap.sstring(1000);
  status                : abap.char(20);
  local_created_by      : abp_creation_user;
  local_created_at      : abp_creation_tstmpl;
  local_last_changed_by : abp_locinst_lastchange_user;
  local_last_changed_at : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl;
  last_changed_at       : abp_lastchange_tstmpl;
}

        5. Save and activate the changes in the table ZRAP_TRAVEL_DATA

2.3 Generate transactional UI service

Create your OData V4-based UI service with the built-in ADT generator. The generated business service will be transactional, draft-enabled, and enriched with UI semantics for the generation of the SAP Fiori elements app.

  1. Right-click your database table ZRAP_TRAVEL_DATA and select Generate ABAP Repository Objects from the context menu.



  2. Select OData UI Service and choose Next and provide the package details.



  3.  Maintain the required information on the Configure Generator dialog to provide the name of your data model and generate them.
  4. For that, navigate through the wizard tree (Business Objects, Data Model, etc…), maintain the artefact names provided and choose Next.
  5. Verify the maintained entries and choose Next to confirm and choose Finish.



  6. The artifacts needed will be generated. Go to the Project Explorer, select your package ZRAP_TRAVEL_ITENERARY, refresh it by choosing F5, and check all generated ABAP repository objects.




    ℹ️ NOTE: The names of the artifacts may differ from those shown in this blog post, as they are generated by the wizard.

2.4 Adjust meta data extensions
Open the metadata extension ZC_RAP_TRAVEL_DATA and make the adjustments as shown in the provided code snippet. ZC_RAP_TRAVEL_DATA should look like this:

@Metadata.layer: #CORE
@UI.headerInfo.title.type: #STANDARD
@UI.headerInfo.title.value: 'TravelID'
@UI.headerInfo.description.type: #STANDARD
@UI.headerInfo.description.value: 'TravelID'

annotate view ZC_RAP_TRAVEL_DATA with
{
  .facet: [
    { label: 'General Information', id: 'GeneralInfo', purpose: #STANDARD, position: 10, type: #IDENTIFICATION_REFERENCE },
    { label: 'AI Generated Itinerary', id: 'AIItineraryFacet', purpose: #STANDARD, position: 200, type: #FIELDGROUP_REFERENCE, targetQualifier: 'AI_ITIN' },
    { label: 'AI Budget Breakdown', id: 'AIBudgetFacet', purpose: #STANDARD, position: 210, type: #FIELDGROUP_REFERENCE, targetQualifier: 'AI_BUDGET' }
  ]

  @EndUserText.label: 'TravelID'
  .identification: [ { position: 10, label: 'TravelID' } ]
  .lineItem: [ { position: 10, label: 'TravelID' } ]
  .selectionField: [ { position: 10 } ]
  TravelID;

  @EndUserText.label: 'TravelerName'
  .identification: [ { position: 20, label: 'TravelerName' } ]
  .lineItem: [ { position: 20, label: 'TravelerName' } ]
  .selectionField: [ { position: 20 } ]
  TravelerName;

  @EndUserText.label: 'StartingPoint'
  .identification: [ { position: 30, label: 'StartingPoint' } ]
  .lineItem: [ { position: 30, label: 'StartingPoint' } ]
  .selectionField: [ { position: 30 } ]
  StartingPoint;

  @EndUserText.label: 'Destination'
  .identification: [ { position: 40, label: 'Destination' } ]
  .lineItem: [ { position: 40, label: 'Destination' } ]
  .selectionField: [ { position: 40 } ]
  Destination;

  .identification: [ { position: 50 } ]
  .lineItem: [ { position: 50 } ]
  .selectionField: [ { position: 50 } ]
  StartDate;

  .identification: [ { position: 60 } ]
  .lineItem: [ { position: 60 } ]
  .selectionField: [ { position: 60 } ]
  EndDate;

  @EndUserText.label: 'Budget'
  .identification: [ { position: 70, label: 'Budget' } ]
  .lineItem: [ { position: 70, label: 'Budget' } ]
  .selectionField: [ { position: 70 } ]
  Budget;

  @EndUserText.label: 'TransportMode'
  .identification: [ { position: 80, label: 'TransportMode' } ]
  .lineItem: [ { position: 80, label: 'TransportMode' } ]
  .selectionField: [ { position: 80 } ]
  TransportMode;

  @EndUserText.label: 'AccommodationType'
  .identification: [ { position: 90, label: 'AccommodationType' } ]
  .lineItem: [ { position: 90, label: 'AccommodationType' } ]
  .selectionField: [ { position: 90 } ]
  AccommodationType;


  // FULL-WIDTH AI ITINERARY
  @EndUserText.label: 'Generated Itinerary'
  .fieldGroup: [ { qualifier: 'AI_ITIN', position: 10, label: 'AI Generated Itinerary' } ]
  .multiLineText: true
  AiItinerary;


  // FULL-WIDTH AI BUDGET
  @EndUserText.label: 'Budget Breakdown'
  .fieldGroup: [ { qualifier: 'AI_BUDGET', position: 10, label: 'AI Budget Breakdown' } ]
  .multiLineText: true
  AiBudgetBreakdown;


  @EndUserText.label: 'Status'
  .identification: [ { position: 120, label: 'Status' } ]
  .lineItem: [ { position: 120, label: 'Status' } ]
  .selectionField: [ { position: 120 } ]
  Status;

   @EndUserText.label: '_BaseEntity'
  .identification: [ {
    position: 180 , 
    label: '_BaseEntity'
  } ]
  .lineItem: [ {
    position: 180 , 
    label: '_BaseEntity'
  } ]
  .selectionField: [ {
    position: 180 
  } ]
  _BaseEntity;

  

}

 2.5 Preview travel app

1. Publish the local service endpoint of your service binding and start the Fiori elements App Preview. Open your service binding ZUI_RAP_TRAVEL_DATA_O4 and choose Publish.

2. Double-click on the Entity ZC_RAP_TRAVEL_DATA in the Entity Set and Association section to open the Fiori elements App Preview.

For reference: Create Database Table and Generate UI Service | SAP Tutorials

3. Create an Intelligent Scenario (Generative AI)  

You first need to create an intelligent scenario for your custom Generative AI use cases. This scenario acts as the central place to manage the entire lifecycle of your AI asset from setup to generating inference results. All lifecycle steps can be executed through the Intelligent Scenario Management app. Here are the key steps to create an intelligent scenario: 

3.1 Create ISLM Scenario
An intelligent scenario is an ABAP representation of a predictive business-specific use case. These intelligent scenarios are AI-powered workflows or processes designed to automate and optimize business tasks.  

  1. Select your package, right-click, New → Other → Intelligent Scenario → ZINTS_RAP120



  2. Enter a name and description. Select SIDEBYSIDE for Scenario Technology field. Choose Next.



  3. Set the following parameters for the created Intelligent Scenario.

Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management supports two primary types of intelligent scenarios:

Embedded Scenarios

Embedded scenarios are built and executed within SAP S/4HANA using the ABAP platform. They rely on the in-database machine learning capabilities of SAP HANA, such as: HANA Predictive Analysis Library (PAL), Automated Predictive Library (APL)

Side-by-Side Scenarios

Side-by-side scenarios, where SAP S/4HANA runs in a separate stack than its machine learning infrastructure provider, such as SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). These scenarios use external ML services or custom ML models.
Generative AI, SAP AI Core, Data Attribute Recommendation

  • For our scenario, we will be using the side by side scenarios type, sap SAPGEN AI
    Turnkey Enablement: Automatically enable SAP-delivered (turnkey) GenAI use cases with minimal configuration effort.

 

Field

Value

Intelligent Scenario Type

SAPGENAI

Automate Turnkey Switch On

Check the checkbox

Usage Type

CUSTOMER

 

  1. Activate the object.

3.2 Create ISLM Model

1. Select your package, right-click, New → Other → Intelligent Scenario Model

2. Fill the Description and Model Name fields. Assign the previously created scenario in Intelligent Scenario Name. Choose Next. 

3. Enter the ExecutableID and Large Language Model Name and Large Language Model Version

 

Field

Possible values

Executable Id

o        aicore-sap

o        aws-bedrock

o        azure-openai

o        gcp-vertexai

Large Language Model Name

It depends on the Executable Id

Large Language Model Version

One option is to leave the version field free, then the latest version will be used. This helps to avoid issues in case that the model has been deprecated in AI Core
 More info: https://me.sap.com/notes/3437766

 

 4. Through the Prompt Library API, we can use predefined prompt templates to generate new prompts. Currently prompt templates are defined through ISLM. To get an instance of the prompt template you want to use, you need to provide the ISLM scenario and the ID of the prompt template. Click on Add and provide the following details: 

Prompt Template Name: SYSTEM_PROMPT_ITIN 
Prompt Template: System prompt for generating Itinerary 
Prompt: You are a professional travel planner. Create a {ISLM_days} day itinerary for a given destination, including daily highlights, must-visit spots and activities. Keep it under 1000 characters. 
 

 5. The Prompt Parameters is captured from the prompt template after activation. 
 

6. Add another prompt template for User prompt:
 
Prompt Template Name: USER_PROMPT_ITIN 
Prompt Template: User prompt template 
Prompt: Destination: {ISLM_destination} Departure: {ISLM_startingpoint} Travel Dates: {ISLM_startdate} to {ISLM_enddate}  Mode of Transport: {ISLM_transportmode} Accommodation Type: {ISLM_accommodationtype} Generate the itinerary now 
  
 

7. Activate the object
⚠ WARNING : Before you try the ABAP AI SDK out in an ABAP Class, please make sure that the deployment is running.  

3.3 In Fiori Launchpad

To use the Intelligent Scenarios and Intelligent Scenario Management apps, you must have the appropriate access rights to the SAP Fiori Launchpad. The access for ISLM is added to the business role of an analytics specialist; BR_ANALYTICS_SPECIALIST.
 You can create and manage ISLM Scenarios through Intelligent Scenario Management app.

You can check your previously created scenario in the Intelligent Scenario Management Fiori App.
In the next step instantiate ABAP AI SDK using the created ISLM Scenario 

3.4. Consume Intelligent Scenarios in ABAP

ABAP AI SDK
The ABAP AI SDK powered by Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM) is an ABAP reuse library that supports you in interacting with large language models (LLMs) hosted on the generative AI hub in SAP AI Core. Using the ABAP AI SDK, you can build your own AI-based features in ABAP.

Completion API
This API provides access to the completion API functionality of Large Language Models (LLMs), meaning that it uses an LLM to generate a text response from a prompt.

Prompt Library API
Using the prompt library API, you can use prompt templates predefined via ISLM to generate new prompts.

Catchable Exceptions
The execution of the completion API may cause an error, and therefore, CX_AIC_COMPLETION_API can be caught as a result. When the error is triggered by the LLM itself or by the AI service provider, CX_AIC_COMPLETION_API provides further information about the error.

Add a determination to the Travel Business Object and enhance it with the ABAP AI SDK powered by ISLM. Define the determination generateAIItinerary in the behavior definition ZR_RAP_TRAVEL_DATA and implement it in the behavior implementation class, aka behavior pool ZBP_R_RAP_TRAVEL_DATA.

1. Add the mandatory fields. Go to the Project Explorer and open your behaviour definition ZR_RAP_TRAVEL_DATA.

field ( readonly )
TravelID,
Status,
AiBudgetBreakdown,
AiItinerary,
LocalCreatedBy,
LocalCreatedAt,
LocalLastChangedBy,
LocalLastChangedAt,
LastChangedAt;

field ( mandatory : create )
TravelerName,
EndDate,
StartDate,
Budget,
AccommodationType,
StartingPoint,
TransportMode,
Destination;

determination generateAIItinerary on modify { create; }

  Define the generateAIItinerary determination:

  1. Save and activate the changes in ZR_RAP_TRAVEL_DATA.
  2. Declare the required method in the behavior implementation class ZBP_RAP_TRAVEL_DATA using ADT Quick Fix  Ctrl/Cmd + 1.

     

     

  3. Save and activate the changes in ZBP_R_RAP_TRAVEL_DATA.
  4. Let's implement the determination generateAIItinerary. In your implementation class ZBP_R_RAP_TRAVEL_DATA, in generateAIItinerary method implementation.
  5. Replace the following code. Your code should look like this:
 METHOD generateAIItinerary.
    LOOP AT keys INTO DATA(ls_key).
      READ ENTITIES OF zr_rap_travel_data IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY ZrRapTravelData
      FIELDS ( destination startdate enddate transportmode accommodationtype     BudgetCurr ) WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
      RESULT DATA(lt_travel).
      LOOP AT lt_travel INTO DATA(ls_travel).
        DATA(days) = ls_travel-enddate - ls_travel-startdate + 1.
        DATA(itinerary) = ``.
        DATA(budget)    = ``.
        IF ls_travel-AiItinerary IS INITIAL OR ls_travel-AiBudgetBreakdown IS INITIAL.
          TRY.

*Calling the Prompt Library API.Through the Prompt Library API, we can use predefined prompt templates to generate new prompts.

              FINAL(sys_prompt_template_instance) =  cl_aic_islm_prompt_tpl_factory=>get( )->create_instance(
                                                   islm_scenario = 'ZINTS_RAP120' template_id = 'SYSTEM_PROMPT_ITIN' ).
              " Add a system prompt based on an ISLM prompt template with one parameter
              FINAL(sys_prompt) = sys_prompt_template_instance->get_prompt( parameters = VALUE #( ( name  = 'ISLM_days'    value = days  ) ) ).

              " Add a user prompt based on an ISLM prompt template with parameters
              FINAL(user_prompt_template_instance) =  cl_aic_islm_prompt_tpl_factory=>get( )->create_instance(
                                                  islm_scenario = 'ZINTS_RAP120' template_id = 'USER_PROMPT_ITIN' ).

              FINAL(user_prompt) = user_prompt_template_instance->get_prompt( parameters = VALUE #(
     ( name  = 'ISLM_startdate'
       value =  |{ ls_travel-startdate }| )
     ( name  = 'ISLM_enddate'
       value = |{ ls_travel-enddate }| )
         ( name  = 'ISLM_startingpoint'
       value = ls_travel-StartingPoint )
           ( name  = 'ISLM_destination'
       value = ls_travel-Destination )
     ( name  = 'ISLM_transportmode'
       value = ls_travel-transportMode )
        ( name  = 'ISLM_accommodationtype'
       value = ls_travel-accommodationtype )
   ) ) .




              FINAL(api_itin) = cl_aic_islm_compl_api_factory=>get( )->create_instance(
                                                      islm_scenario = 'ZINTS_RAP120' ).

*Set the LLM parameters
              FINAL(params) = api_itin->get_parameter_setter( ).
              params->set_maximum_tokens( 800 ).


              FINAL(msg_itin) = api_itin->create_message_container( ).
              msg_itin->set_system_role( sys_prompt ).
              msg_itin->add_user_message( user_prompt ).
              itinerary = api_itin->execute_for_messages( msg_itin )->get_completion( ).
            CATCH cx_aic_api_factory INTO DATA(lx_api_itin).
              itinerary = |AI instance creation failed for itinerary.|.
            CATCH cx_aic_completion_api INTO DATA(lx_comp_itin).
              itinerary = |AI completion call failed for itinerary.|.
          ENDTRY.
          IF itinerary IS NOT INITIAL.
            TRY.

* To pass prompts directly without prompt parameters
* Your ISLM scenario for itinerary

                DATA(system_prompt_budget) =
                  |You are a travel finance advisor. Based on the Itenary , destination, duration, | &&
                  |and total budget, estimate how much to allocate for travel, hotel, | &&
                  |food, sightseeing, and miscellaneous expenses. Output under 500 characters.|.
                DATA(user_prompt_budget) =
                  |Itenerary: { itinerary }| &&
                  |Departure: { ls_travel-StartingPoint }| &&
                 |Destination: { ls_travel-destination }| &&
                  |\nTravel Dates: { ls_travel-startdate } to { ls_travel-enddate }| &&
                  |\nTotal Budget: ₹{ ls_travel-Budget }| &&
                  |\nTotal Budget Currency: ₹{ ls_travel-BudgetCurr }| &&
                  |\nProvide a short, realistic breakdown.|.
                FINAL(api_budget) = cl_aic_islm_compl_api_factory=>get( )->create_instance(
                                                      islm_scenario = 'ZINTS_RAP120' ).
                " Your ISLM scenario for budget
                FINAL(msg_budget) = api_budget->create_message_container( ).
                msg_budget->set_system_role( system_prompt_budget ).
                msg_budget->add_user_message( user_prompt_budget ).
                budget = api_budget->execute_for_messages( msg_budget )->get_completion( ).
              CATCH cx_aic_api_factory INTO DATA(lx_api_budget).
                budget = |AI instance creation failed for budget.|.
              CATCH cx_aic_completion_api INTO DATA(lx_comp_budget).
                budget = |AI completion call failed for budget.|.
            ENDTRY.
          ENDIF.
          MODIFY ENTITIES OF zr_rap_travel_data  IN LOCAL MODE
            ENTITY ZrRapTravelData
           UPDATE FIELDS ( aiitinerary aibudgetbreakdown status )
            WITH VALUE #(
              ( %tky = ls_travel-%tky
                aiitinerary        = itinerary
                aibudgetbreakdown = budget
                status              = 'Generated' )
            ).
        ENDIF.
      ENDLOOP.
    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.
  1. Save and activate the changes in ZBP_R_RAP_TRAVEL_DATA.
  2. Preview and test the enhanced Travel Itinerary App with Generative AI Capabilities.

 

 

 

For the latest updates, subscribe to the blog posts below : 

ABAP AI - Chapter 2 - SAP Community
Introducing the Next Era of ABAP Development - SAP Community
Joule speaks ABAP! - SAP Community
From Legacy to AI-Powered: RISE Transformation Rev... - SAP Community

Happy Learning!

entry.title: AI in SAP SuccessFactors (2026) — The Adoption Operating Model
entry.content:
Part 3 of the “Practical Implementation Blueprint” series

Goal of this blog: Help you run AI in SuccessFactors like a product you operate, not a feature you enable once, so adoption stays trusted, measurable, and release-aligned.

Continuation from Part 1 & Part 2

In Part 1, we covered readiness, governance, identity/permissions, and the activation roadmap across Joule, embedded AI, and extensibility. In Part 2, we focused on the change blueprint: leadership ownership, frontline manager enablement, and operationalizing “AI suggests; humans decide”.

Now Part 3 answers what customers hit after go-live:
How do we run AI like a product -- safely, predictably, and aligned to SAP releases : without chaos, adoption drop-off, or budget surprises?

Series navigation: If you’re just starting with this blog, I recommend reading the first two posts first, then continuing here with Part 3.

  • Part 1 — AI in SAP SuccessFactors (2026): The Practical Implementation Blueprint
  • Part 2 — AI in SAP SuccessFactors (2026): The Change Management Blueprint
  • Part 3 — AI in SAP SuccessFactors (2026): The Adoption Operating Model (this blog) 😊

Executive Summary (2-minute read)

The challenge: Many AI initiatives fail to scale because operating ownership, risk controls, and value tracking aren’t designed for continuous AI change. Gartner has predicted that a significant portion of agentic AI projects will be cancelled due to cost, unclear value, or insufficient risk controls.

The solution: Treat AI as a product you operate, not a feature you “turn on once.”

What you'll get

  • A minimum viable COE with clear accountability (and practical time allocations)
  • A three-tier governance cadence that stays agile without losing control
  • A single intake model that stops “random cool ideas”
  • A release-aligned quality loop so accuracy doesn’t erode over time
  • AI Units governance patterns using SAP usage metrics + dashboards
  • EU AI Act–aligned literacy + employment-risk handling patterns

Time to value (practical guidance)

  • 4–8 weeks to stand up basic governance + intake + dashboards
  • 3–6 months to stabilize the quality loop and embed monthly steering rhythms
  • 6–12 months to reach predictable “product mode” cadence aligned to releases

0) Why an operating model is required in 2026

AI in SuccessFactors is not static. You’re operating in a world of:

  • Joule capabilities evolving and relying on correct setup and permissions (for example, “Access to Joule” prerequisites).
  • Premium AI consumption monitoring through AI Units usage metrics and dashboards.
  • EU AI Act obligations, including AI literacy measures (Article 4 + EU Q&A).
  • Employment-related risk handling, because employment/worker management use cases are explicitly highlighted under Annex III (recruitment/selection and worker-management contexts).

That’s why mature customers shift from “AI rollout project” to an “AI product operating model.”

The 5 most common failure patterns (and how this blog prevents them)

Failure pattern
What happensPrevention strategy (where in this blog)
1) Orphaned ownership“Everyone owns AI” = no one owns it. Backlog becomes political. Adoption becomes inconsistent.Section 2 (COE roles + time allocation), Section 3 (cadence + decision rights)
2) Permission driftRBP/security changes break Joule journeys; users perceive “AI is broken.”Section 1.2, Section 5 (RBP impact checks + regression)
3) Random innovationMany custom skills/agents get built without lifecycle, test, support, or retirement plan.Section 4 (single intake + scorecard + decision tree)
4) Budget shockConsumption spikes appear when scaling cohorts or launching high-usage scenarios.Section 7 (monthly governance + spike diagnostic)
5) Quality erosionAccuracy and usefulness degrade release-over-release; no one notices until trust collapses.Section 6 (weekly quality loop + classification + fixes)

1) Operating model principles (2026 rules of the road)

1.1 One program, three AI surfaces

AI shows up in three interconnected layers:

  • Joule (assistant experience and guided support)
  • Embedded AI (AI-assisted experiences inside modules)
  • Extensibility (Joule Studio / SAP Build) for custom agents and integrations

Operating model implication: One intake, one backlog, one cadence, one measurement framework.

Illustrative example (common pattern): Organizations that consolidate Joule, embedded AI, and custom skills under one Product Owner and one backlog typically reduce duplicated initiatives and clarify the “which AI to use when” confusion faster than split governance models.

1.2 Security and permissions must remain “in the room” forever

Joule experiences depend on correct setup and permissions. As a result, security changes can “break” AI journeys even when AI itself is fine.

Concrete example of impact (permission drift):

  • You enable a manager-facing journey that relies on viewing team data.
  • Security/HR later tightens RBP visibility.
  • Result: managers hit errors or incomplete answers and conclude: “AI is broken.”

Prevention checklist

  • [ ] Security/Privacy has a permanent seat in Product Council (Section 3)
  • [ ] Every RBP change includes an AI journey impact assessment
  • [ ] Regression includes your top 10 AI-supported workflows (Section 5)
  • [ ] Support team has a “Permission vs AI” diagnostic script (Section 6)

1.3 Human-in-the-loop maturity progression

Part 2 made this clear: “AI suggests; humans decide.” Part 3 operationalizes it with maturity:

  • Level 1 (Basic): Generic disclaimer (“review output”).
  • Level 2 (Functional): Use case–specific guidance (e.g., drafting content requires compliance review).
  • Level 3 (Optimized): Contextual guardrails at decision points + manager coaching routines + auditable workflow markers (e.g., “AI assisted” indicator) for sensitive contexts.

1.4 AI literacy maturity model (EU-aligned, practical globally)

EU AI Act Article 4 expects organizations to take measures to ensure sufficient AI literacy for staff using AI systems. Treat literacy as onboarding + refresh + role-based deepening for high-impact roles.

Level
All employeesManagersHigh-impact roles (Recruiting, ER, Performance)
FoundationWhat AI can/can’t do; basic safety; where to find help+ HITL responsibilities; escalation paths+ Regulatory context; documentation expectations
PracticedQuarterly tips / refreshersManager community of practiceScenario-based practice
AdvancedSelf-service help centerCoaching othersAudit readiness + policy updates

2) AI COE structure with time allocations (minimum viable)

You don’t need a giant COE. You need clear accountability.

Role
OwnerTime allocation (guidance)Primary accountability
AI Product OwnerHR/HRIT50–60% FTEOutcomes, backlog priorities, adoption targets
SuccessFactors Platform OwnerHRIT30–40% FTEConfiguration, RBP changes, release impact, regression
BTP / Integration OwnerIT20–30% initially (higher if heavy Joule Studio use)Connectivity, extensibility lifecycle
Security + PrivacyCISO/DPO rep10–15% FTEPolicy, access risk, compliance sign-offs
Change & Enablement LeadHR Comms/L&D30–40% FTEComms, manager readiness, AI literacy plan
AI Knowledge/Content CuratorCOE/Ops20–30% FTEApproved sources, quality loop fixes
Adoption & Insights AnalystHRIT/HR Analytics20–25% FTEDashboards, ticket deflection, failure analysis

Sizing guidance (practical)

  • Small (<5,000 employees): combine roles; ~2 FTE total
  • Medium (5,000–25,000): ~3.5–5 FTE
  • Large (25,000+): ~5–8 FTE initially; higher at maturity depending on custom agents scope

3) Governance cadence with agendas (make it boring and repeatable)

Tier A — Executive Steering (Monthly/Quarterly)

Duration: 60 minutes

Standard agenda

  1. Adoption dashboard review (15 min)
  2. Risk register + top 3 issues (15 min)
  3. AI Units consumption vs budget (10 min)
  4. Value delivered vs business case (15 min)
  5. Go/no-go decisions (5 min)

Tier B — AI Product Council (Biweekly, 60 min)

Standard agenda

  1. Usage trends + top issues from last sprint (15 min)
  2. Intake review: new requests + prioritization (20 min)
  3. In-flight initiatives: status + blockers (15 min)
  4. Next cycle planning: what goes live (10 min)

Decision aids

  • Prioritization scorecard (Section 4.2)
  • Quality loop summary (Section 6)
  • Capacity planning + AI Units budget view

Tier C — AI Ops Review (Weekly, 30 min)

Standard agenda

  1. Hot issues from last 7 days (10 min)
  2. Quality loop: top 5 feedback themes (10 min)
  3. Quick wins for this week (5 min)
  4. Escalations + handoffs (5 min)

Practical embedding of managers

  • Rotate 2–3 manager representatives through Product Council quarterly
  • Monthly “manager listening sessions” (30 min) to capture friction
  • Include manager feedback scores in Tier A dashboard

4) Intake and prioritization (stop “random cool ideas”)

4.1 Single intake form (required fields)

  • Persona + workflow
  • Value hypothesis + metric
  • Data sensitivity
  • Human-in-the-loop requirement (yes/no + why)
  • Operational cost (support impact)
  • AI Units estimate + monitoring plan

4.2 Prioritization scorecard (weighted, transparent)

Dimension
12345Weight
Business valueMinimal impactNice-to-haveClear benefitHigh valueStrategic3x
FrequencyQuarterlyMonthlyWeeklyDailyMultiple/day2x
ReadinessMajor gapsSome gapsMostly readyReadyPlug-and-play2x
Risk/ComplianceHigh riskModerateStandardLowMinimal-2x
Consumption/CostVery highHighModerateLowMinimal-1x

Priority score formula

Priority Score = [(Value × 3) + (Frequency × 2) + (Readiness × 2)] - [(Risk × 2) + (Cost × 1)]
Maximum possible: 35
Minimum possible: -15

Prioritization thresholds

  • 25+ = Fast track (next sprint)
  • 15–24 = Standard backlog (sequence by strategic fit)
  • 10–14 = Consider (needs strengthening or cost reduction)
  • <10 = Defer or redesign

4.3 Intake decision tree (edge cases)

NEW REQUEST RECEIVED
│
├─ Is this employment-related (recruiting, performance decisions, termination)?
│  ├─ YES → Risk = 4 or 5 + Security/Privacy review required
│  └─ NO → Continue
│
├─ Do we have 3+ similar requests from different teams?
│  ├─ YES → Consolidate into one initiative (avoid duplication)
│  └─ NO → Continue
│
├─ Does this require NEW data sources or integrations?
│  ├─ YES → IT/BTP Owner assesses feasibility first (may delay 1–2 sprints)
│  └─ NO → Continue
│
├─ Is estimated AI Units cost >20% of monthly budget?
│  ├─ YES → Requires Tier A approval before piloting
│  └─ NO → Product Council can approve
│
└─ OUTCOME: Score using Section 4.2 + add to backlog with priority band

5) Release alignment playbook (SuccessFactors + AI)

Treat each SuccessFactors release as an AI change event: permissions/config shifts can break journeys, embedded behaviors can change, and custom agents need regression.

5.1 Release impact example (illustrative)

If permissions for a manager process change but your regression doesn’t include “Joule + that process,” you can generate a wave of tickets and a trust hit that takes weeks to recover.

5.2 Minimum release checklist (copy/paste)

  • Preview: identify impacted AI journeys; regression (top workflows + custom agents); refresh enablement (“what changed” + safe scope)
  • Go-live + 2 weeks: daily monitoring (tickets + wrong/confusing themes); rapid fixes (content, guidance, permissions); escalate defects to SAP if needed
  • Post-release: reprioritize backlog; update manager starter tasks (Part 2) with new value

6) The quality loop (how you reduce wrong/confusing outputs over time)

The goal is not perfection on day one. The goal is a repeatable improvement mechanism.

6.1 Weekly loop: Capture → Classify → Fix → Validate

Step 2: CLASSIFY (weekly)

Issue type
DescriptionExampleTypical fix
A) Missing/unclear contentPolicy/process info missing or outdated“Can’t find updated parental leave policy”Update knowledge source
B) Permission boundaryUser expects access they don’t have“Manager tries to view data outside role”Fix RBP OR clarify guidance
C) Low confidence/misinterpretationWrong/unclear response“Misread internal acronym”Add clarifying content; narrow scope
D) Product defectPlatform behavior/error“Error with valid permission”Escalate to SAP support
E) Misuse/unrealistic expectationUser asks AI to do unsafe things“Draft termination letter”Strengthen guardrails + enablement

6.3 Example quality improvement (before & after)

Scenario: Joule helps users find learning opportunities.

  • Before: results too broad; users say “not helpful.”
  • Actions: improve learning paths content + add guidance on better phrasing + apply scope rules.
  • After: users find relevant results faster; repeat usage rises; you scale with updated guidance.

7) AI Units governance (prevent budget surprises)

SAP provides AI Units usage metrics and consumption summaries so you can operationalize consumption governance. You should monitor consumption alongside adoption and value.

7.1 Minimum controls

  • Assign an AI Consumption Owner
  • Monthly consumption review: actual vs planned, spikes by cohort/process, 90-day forecast and guardrails
  • Scaling rule: no new cohort expansion until consumption trend is stable (or budget approved)

7.2 Monthly dashboard (Consumption + Value together)

Track consumption next to value so finance doesn’t only see “cost,” and HR doesn’t only see “adoption.”

Metric
This monthLast month3-mo avgTarget
AI Units consumed[_____][_____][_____][_____]
Cost per active user[_____][_____][_____][_____]
Tickets deflected[_____][_____][_____][_____]
Ticket deflection value*[_____][_____][_____][_____]
Net value (deflection − AI cost)[_____][_____][_____][_____]

*Use your internal cost-per-ticket assumption (e.g., service desk fully loaded cost).

7.3 Consumption spike diagnostic (runbook)

SPIKE DETECTED (>20% month-over-month)
│
├─ New cohort added this month?
│  ├─ YES → Expected. Monitor for next 30 days for stabilization.
│  └─ NO → Investigate further ↓
│
├─ New use case launched?
│  ├─ YES → Check if usage is appropriate. May need guardrails.
│  └─ NO → Investigate further ↓
│
├─ Seasonal pattern (e.g., year-end reviews, open enrollment)?
│  ├─ YES → Expected. Plan for next year.
│  └─ NO → Investigate further ↓
│
├─ Heavy user outliers (top 10% using >5x average)?
│  ├─ YES → Interview users:
│  │     • High-value power users? → Good. Feature in case study.
│  │     • Misuse or bot-like behavior? → Restrict or re-train.
│  └─ NO → Escalate to SAP (possible platform anomaly)

Compliance layer (EU focus, strong practice globally)

8.1 AI literacy (Article 4) must be a run-state deliverable

Treat literacy as onboarding + refresh + role-based deepening for high-impact roles. Integrate it into existing learning journeys (e.g., new manager onboarding) rather than creating isolated “AI training.”

Audience 
Initial (onboarding)Ongoing (run-state)High-impact (annual+)
All employees15-min module: what AI can/can’t do; where to find it; basic safetyQuarterly tips + refresh for new featuresSelf-service help center
All managers+ 30-min module: HITL responsibilities; escalation; “AI suggests; humans decide”Manager community of practice (quarterly)Scenario-based refresher
High-impact roles
(Recruiting, Performance, ER)		+ 60-min module: regulatory context; documentation expectations; bias awarenessQuarterly scenario practice + policy updatesAudit readiness workshop

8.2 Employment-related AI use cases require special handling

Maintain an AI Use Case Register for employment/worker-management contexts: classification, controls (HITL, documentation), sign-offs, and change history.

9) Measurement: ATVS dashboard (Adoption, Trust, Value, Safety)

Adoption

  • Active users (7/30)
  • Repeat usage trend

Trust

  • “I understand what’s safe”
  • “I know how to escalate”

Value

  • Ticket deflection
  • Time-to-complete improvements for top HR tasks

Safety

  • Permission boundary incidents
  • Register coverage for employment-related use cases

10) Visual framework: how it all connects

INTAKE → PRIORITIZE → PILOT → QUALITY LOOP → SCALE
   ↓          ↓          ↓          ↓           ↓
Product    Tier B     Tier C      Weekly     Tier A
Council   Decision    Review      Ops        Approval
   ↑          ↑          ↑          ↑           ↑
   └──────────┴──────────┴──────────┴───────────┘
              GOVERNANCE CADENCE
                     ↕
           RELEASE ALIGNMENT (each release)
                     ↕
         MEASUREMENT (ATVS Dashboard)

Key dependencies

  • Security/Privacy sits across governance tiers
  • AI Units monitored at Tier C, reviewed at Tier B, and steered/approved at Tier A
  • Quality loop feeds Product Council priorities
  • Release alignment triggers regression testing across all active use cases

11) AI operating model maturity levels

Level 1 — Initial (Months 0–3)

  • Basic COE roles assigned
  • Governance forums established
  • First pilots running
  • Quality loop mostly reactive
  • Ad-hoc consumption monitoring

Level 2 — Defined (Months 4–6)

  • Formal intake process
  • Prioritization scorecard in use
  • Weekly quality loop operational
  • Release regression testing begins
  • Not all use cases have HITL clarity yet

Level 3 — Managed (Months 7–12)

  • Predictable consumption forecasting
  • Value metrics tracked consistently
  • Manager enablement scaled (from Part 2)
  • Proactive quality improvements
  • Release process becomes predictable

Level 4 — Optimized (12+ months)

  • AI embedded in HR operating rhythm
  • Self-sustaining quality loop
  • Consumption + value balanced
  • Release process predictable
  • Expanding to new modules confidently

Your practical goal: Reach Level 3 by month 9–12.

12) Templates (copy/paste)

12.1 COE RACI (starter)

  • AI Product Owner — outcomes/backlog/adoption
  • SF Platform Owner — configuration/RBP/release regression
  • BTP / Joule Studio Owner — custom agent lifecycle
  • Security/Privacy — risk acceptance + sign-offs
  • Enablement Lead — literacy + communications
  • Knowledge Curator — source quality loop fixes
  • Support Lead — incident/problem/change coordination

12.2 Intake form (starter)

  • Title + owner
  • Persona + workflow
  • Value metric
  • Safe scope + HITL
  • Data sensitivity
  • AI Units estimate + monitoring plan
  • Pilot cohort
  • Definition of Done

12.3 Release checklist (starter)

  • Review “What’s New” items impacting Joule/AI journeys
  • Regression: top journeys + custom agents
  • Enablement refresh (what changed + safe scope)
  • Support script update
  • Post-release monitoring plan

If you enjoyed this series, I’d really appreciate a kudo, it really encourages me to keep writing more blogs 😊

Final note

In 2026, the goal isn’t “AI go-live.” The goal is habit + trust + measurable value : maintained through an operating rhythm. If you treat AI as a product you operate (intake, backlog, governance, release alignment, quality loop, and ATVS measurement), you significantly increase your chance of scaling impact without losing control of risk or cost.

entry.title: Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
Welcome to your one-stop shop to learn and share knowledge about SAP's ready-to-run public cloud ERP software solution.  

SAP Cloud ERP is part of SAP Business Suite that brings AI, data, and applications together into a fully integrated system. This community is open for anyone in the Cloud ERP ecosystem–customers, partners, and SAP experts.  

 

In this blog post I’d like to outline the innovations for SAP Cloud ERP 2602 in the area of Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management. These are the topics that are covered:

  • Enhanced integration of project plan information with SAP Cloud ERP (6JN) 
  • Project staffing view for resource managers (6JO) 
  • Allowing contingent workers (cost center procured) to record time on enterprise projects (1NT, 1Q4) 
  • Automatic removal of billing block for sales order billing plans (1NT) 
  • Joule as project assistant (beta), planned to be available mid-March
  • Further innovations

 

Check out my video to get a glimpse of the new features:

 

In today's fast-paced business environment, running projects efficiently, on time, and on budget is more critical than ever. This release is packed with features designed to bring more intelligence, automation, and transparency to your projects, ultimately driving significant business value. We’ve focused on streamlining processes, enhancing integration, and even giving you a glimpse into the future with AI-powered assistance.

Let's dive into the highlights that will help you unlock new levels of project success.

 

Enhancements in SAP Project and Resource Management

 

Enhanced integration of project plan information with SAP Cloud ERP

In complex projects spanning engineering, manufacturing, and services, a major challenge is keeping project execution plans in sync with core financial and logistical data. A delay in a design phase, for example, has a direct impact on procurement timelines and billing dates. If this information isn't shared instantly between the project planning tool and the core ERP, decisions are made based on outdated information. To solve this, our 2602 release supercharges the integration between SAP Project and Resource Management, our solution on the SAP Business Technology Platform, and SAP Cloud ERP. Now, when a project manager adjusts a project plan – such as extending a phase – the system automatically synchronizes these date changes with the core ERP in real time.

This direct synchronization ensures that your financial, commercial, and logistical data within SAP Cloud ERP always reflects the most current project reality, empowering you to make reliable decisions based on a single source of truth. By automating the update process, you eliminate the need for project teams to manually replicate date changes across systems, which significantly reduces administrative effort and minimizes the risk of human error. This tight integration also lays the groundwork for faster billing cycles, as milestone completions in the project plan can now directly trigger commercial processes in the core system.

 

Figure 1: Improved integration of project plan information between SAP Cloud ERP and SAP Project and Resource Management, enables better decision-making in projects through reliable, up-to-date project plan information.

 

Project staffing view for resource managers

Resource managers are constantly juggling demands from multiple projects, and gaining a clear, consolidated overview of the staffing situation for a specific project can be a fragmented and time-consuming process. To address this pain point, we are introducing a brand-new SAP Fiori app called “Manage Request Assignments”. This powerful application is designed specifically for resource managers, providing a single, project-centric view of all resource requests. It shows at a glance which roles have been fully staffed, which are partially staffed, and which are still open, eliminating the need to dig through multiple screens and reports.

This new application provides resource managers with unprecedented transparency into the staffing health of any project, allowing them to instantly identify bottlenecks and open demands without extensive research. Having all the necessary information in one place, with direct navigation to take action, streamlines the workflow and dramatically reduces the time it takes to staff open requests and get the right people on the right tasks. Ultimately, a clear, holistic overview empowers resource managers to more effectively optimize staffing within a single project or re-allocate experts across multiple projects, ensuring your most valuable resources are deployed with maximum impact.

 

Figure 2: The “Manage Request Assignments” application allows users to efficiently view the resource request and assignment details at a project level.

 

Other Innovations in SAP Project and Resource Management

Additional enhancements introduced in SAP Project and Resource Management. For example:

  • Technical user flow coverage (client credential flow) for some APIs
  • Use of actual dates in scheduling
  • Consideration of working and none working days in scheduling
  • Ability to block certain file types during file upload
  • Additional details for tasks and collaboration project

For a complete list and more details please take a look into the What's new document for SAP Project and Resource Management

 

 

Enhancements in Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management

 

Allowing contingent workers (cost center procured) to record time on enterprise projects

Modern project teams are a dynamic blend of internal employees and external contingent workers. A significant process gap existed for contingent workers who were procured via a cost center, as they couldn't use the standard “My Timesheet” application to record time against enterprise projects. This made it difficult to get a complete and timely picture of all labor hours and costs. With the 2602 release, we have closed this gap. Now, these contingent workers can use the very same “My Timesheet” application as internal employees to record their time against the projects they are staffed on, creating a unified experience for the entire team.

This enhancement delivers a unified and simplified process where all project resources, regardless of their employment type, use a single application for time recording, which improves the user experience. You can now capture every hour worked against your project demands in one central place, providing a more accurate and timely view of total project costs and progress. Furthermore, the system automates the creation of the corresponding service entry sheet and goods receipt upon timesheet approval, significantly reducing administrative overhead and ensuring your suppliers are paid accurately and on time.

 

Figure 3: Project-based and cost center-based contingent workers can record time seamlessly in the “Manage My Timesheet” application, ensuring consistent tracking and better visibility across all engagement types.

 

Automatic removal of billing block for sales order billing plans

Milestone billing is a fantastic way to align payments with project progress, but it has often been hampered by a manual step. Once a milestone was achieved, someone had to go into the sales order and manually remove the billing block to make that item available for invoicing, a delay that directly impacts cash flow. We have now fully automated this process. Building on a feature from our last release, the system now automatically removes the billing block from the corresponding billing plan item in the related sales order the moment a project manager confirms the completion of that milestone in the project.

By completely automating this process, you eliminate the manual hand-off and inherent delays, allowing you to invoice your customers the very moment a milestone is contractually met. This directly accelerates your order-to-cash cycle and improves cash flow. This automation also removes a tedious and error-prone manual task, freeing up your project controllers and sales administrators to focus their expertise on more strategic, value-added activities. The risk of human error is entirely eliminated, leading to a smoother, more reliable, and more accurate billing process from end to end.

 

Figure 4: For sales order billing plan items which are created based on enterprise project or WBS element milestones, the billing block is automatically removed when the related milestone is completed.

 

Joule as project assistant (beta), planned to be available mid-March

Setting up a new project can be a complex and time-consuming task, often requiring project managers to navigate between three or four different applications to define the scope, staff resources, and release the project for time recording. We are incredibly excited to give you a preview of how we are solving this with the future of project management: the Joule Project Assistant. This is our generative AI copilot, and we are starting with a beta agent designed to radically simplify the creation of internal projects. Instead of clicking through multiple screens, a project manager can simply ask Joule in natural language, e.g. "Create a new pre-sales project for the 'Rising Star' initiative, starting next week and lasting for one month.". Joule can then propose a template, adjust dates, staff the project, and release it – all within a single, conversational session.

This AI-powered assistant promises dramatic efficiency gains, condensing a multi-application process into a single, swift conversation that saves significant time and effort for your project managers. The intuitive, conversational nature of Joule makes project creation accessible to everyone, drastically reducing the learning curve and empowering users to initiate projects quickly and correctly. By leveraging templates from successful past projects, the Joule agent also helps ensure that new projects are set up consistently and in compliance with your company's standards right from the start. We are currently running a beta program for this and encourage you to get involved! Register for the Customer Engagement Initiative via this landing page as of March 15, 2026.

 

Figure 5: The AI-powered solution assists users in rapidly setting up projects by leveraging similar past projects when the volume is high, and projects are repetitive in nature.



Other innovations in Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management

We know how critical it is to have a clear, unified view of project financials. That's why we’ve introduced the company code currency into the Project Cost report. Now, you can see a consolidated view of all your key cost figures including planned costs, actuals, commitments, and even project stock costs – all in a single, consistent currency. This provides a much clearer picture of your project's overall financial health and variance.

There is one important prerequisite to keep in mind: for the report to display in the company code currency, all the projects and WBS elements you select for that report must share the same company code. This ensures data integrity and accurate reporting. For a more detailed guide on this feature, I encourage you to check out the documentation on the SAP Help Portal.

Second, we’ve Improved system usability by segregating the profitability segment from settlement rules for clearer allocation and analysis. What does this mean for you? In the past, assigning profitability might have required you to set up a settlement rule. Now, for projects with revenue, you can assign the profitability segment directly to a billing WBS element using the "Assign" function. This is a much more direct and intuitive workflow. It saves you steps, simplifies the allocation process, and makes your analysis clearer and more efficient. As with all our features, you can find comprehensive details on this enhancement in the SAP Help Portal.

Finally, and this is something I’m personally very excited about, we are going beyond system features to build a stronger community. We are officially introducing the new CIP community – a collaborative space designed for our customers, partners, and SAP experts to connect. The goal is to build a powerful network where we can all share ideas, discuss best practices, and co-innovate on the future of enterprise and collaborative project scenarios. This is your platform to influence the direction of our products and learn from your peers.

If you are interested in joining this community and helping shape the future of project management at SAP, please reach out to my colleagues, Anke Junker anke.junker@sap.com or Varsha Chetan Manu varsha.chetan.manu@sap.com, to get involved.


The SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release for EPPM is all about making your project management more connected, automated, and intelligent. From ensuring a single source of truth to simplifying daily tasks for every project role, these innovations are designed to deliver tangible business value and set you up for success.

Thank you for your time, and I look forward to seeing how you leverage these new capabilities.

 

 

📽️ Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series!

From January 12-16, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find the recording and presentation for Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management as well as 21 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand – anywhere, anytime.

 

🔎Find all our assets

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: 【SAPパートナー必見】Joule for Consultants(J4C)検討の完全ガイド
entry.content:
こちらのブログは、Finn Backerさんのこちらのブログを参考に作成したものです。

― 2026年より、Development Fundの活用も可能に ―

【2026年1月15日アップデート】
「SAP Development Fund（DF）を使って、J4C向けのAI Unitsを購入できるのか？」という質問を多くいただいています。結論から言うと、2026年より可能になりました。詳細は本文をご覧ください。

Joule for Consultants（J4C）が、すべてのパートナーに提供開始

Joule for Consultants（以下 J4C）は、SAPが昨年にローンチした Per User Per Month（PUPM）価格体系の一部として、すべてのSAPパートナーが購入・利用可能になりました。

すでにSAP社内のコンサルタントは長期間にわたってJ4Cを活用しており、
生産性向上・ナレッジアクセスの高速化・品質向上といった大きな効果が報告されています。（詳細はこちらの資料の7〜9ページをご参照ください）
日本語資料は、じゃぱぽ Business AIページからご確認ください。
(じゃぱぽへ初めてアクセスされる方は、こちらからご登録ください)

特に注目すべきは、
小規模パートナーでも大きな初期投資なしで始められる価格設計になっている点です。

パートナーからよく聞かれる5つの質問

Q1. SAP AI Unitsはどうやって購入しますか？

方法は主に2つあります。

① SAP Storeから購入（条件付き）

以下の条件を満たす場合のみ可能です：

  • 過去に 旧AI Unitを購入したことがない

  • 購入履歴が SKU 8019164 のみ

該当する場合は、SAP Storeで「Buy as a customer」を選択して購入できます。

② SAPのAccount Executive（AE）経由で購入

  • SAP AE に見積を依頼

  • Partner Business Manager（PBM）に相談してもOK

必要なAI Units数の目安

  • J4C：1ユーザーあたり 月35 AI Units

  • 例：1ユーザー × 12か月 = 420 AI Units

  • AI Unitsは100単位販売のため → 500 AI Units の購入が必要

Q2. Joule for ConsultantsにTDD（Test / Development / Demo）はありますか？

ありません。理由は明確です。

TDDは本来、

「パートナーが顧客向けSAPソリューションを検証・デモするための環境」

一方J4Cは、

パートナー自身が“本番業務”で使うツール

つまり、
パートナー自身がSAPの“顧客”として利用する位置づけになります。
そのため、J4Cは 本番（Productive）価格表のみで提供されています。

Q3. SAP Development Fund（DF）を使ってJ4Cを購入できますか？

はい。できます。

2026年1月より、SAP Development Fundを使って
J4C向けのSAP AI Units購入費用の一部をリインバース可能になりました。
ただし、SAP Development Fundが利用可能なパートナー限定となります。

⚠️ 重要な注意点

  • AI Units購入前に、必ず PBMへ事前相談・事前承認を行ってください

  • DF適用条件や手続きはパートナーごとに異なるため、詳細はPBMに確認してください

Q4. J4Cを使うために、SAPの業務システムを本番稼働させる必要はありますか？

不要です。

J4Cは スタンドアロンで動作します。

  • SAP BTPのサブアカウント上にインストール

  • コンサルタントは 専用URL から直接アクセス

  • SAPの他の本番／TDDシステムは不要

Q5. グローバルで1つのJ4Cを使えますか？それとも拠点ごとに必要？

グローバルで1インスタンス運用が可能です。J4Cは他システムと独立しているため、

  • グローバルBTP上で1インスタンス運用

  • 世界中のコンサルタントが同じURLにアクセス

ただし、

  • IAS（Identity Authentication Service）でのユーザー管理は必要です

J4Cの最適な利用対象者は？

主なターゲットは以下です：

  • 機能コンサルタント

  • 技術コンサルタント

  • SAP導入・運用支援を行うコンサルタント

活用例

  • ジュニアコンサルタント：
    → シニアに聞かずに即時に知識取得、学習スピード向上

  • シニアコンサルタント：
    → ピンポイントな情報検索、新領域対応、最新情報のキャッチアップ

J4Cを顧客に販売することはできますか？

はい、可能です。

  • パートナーは SAP AI Units（SKU 8019164） を顧客に販売可能

  • 顧客は SAP for Me からJ4Cのアクティベーションを実施

社内IT部門やパワーユーザーを抱える顧客にとっても、大きな価値があります。

まとめ：すべてのSAPパートナーが、今すぐ始められる

Joule for Consultantsは、

  • 小さく始められる

  • 月単位で柔軟に使える

  • DFも活用できる

  • グローバル展開も可能

すべてのSAPパートナーにとって、現実的かつ効果的な投資対象です。

導入にあたっては、以下を理解しておくことが重要です：

  • AI Unitsの購入方法

  • J4Cを稼働させるBTP環境の選定

  • SAP for Meでのアクティベーション

  • AI Unitsの消費モデル

  • SAP BTP上でのセットアップ方法

これらの詳細な手順については、こちらのガイド資料をご参照ください。

entry.title: How AI Automation Eliminates Master Data Duplicates in SAP
entry.content:
The Business Case

Whether running SAP ECC or S/4HANA, most organizations struggle with duplicate master data for materials, vendors, and customers. This isn't just a "clutter" problem, poor material master data leads to users creating Purchase Requisitions with text items, bypassing procurement controls and ruining spend analytics.

Let’s explore how AI automation and data vectorization can clean up the core and prevent duplicates before they happen.

The Engine: What is Data Vectorization?

At Skybuffer, we are constantly exploring the benefits of SAP data vectorization. Simply put, vectorization converts data into numerical arrays (vectors) using embedding functions.

Unlike standard SAP keyword searches, vectors are language-independent and allow algorithms to perform similarity searches. While standard tables see rows and columns, a vectorized model sees the relationship between data points.

How Does It Help?

When data is vectorized, we can execute hybrid searches that are indifferent to word order or exact spelling.

Example: Imagine searching for a global customer like "Skybuffer." You might have different accounts for Poland and Norway. If a user tries to create a new record, they can simply ask:

"Do we have a Customer master record for Skybuffer in Poland?"

By querying vectorized KNA1 table data, the AI identifies the record even if the naming convention differs slightly.

 Pic.1 Hybrid Search based on Vectorized KNA1 Table Data

Real-World Integration Points

Hybrid search is most powerful when embedded directly into the SAP business process:

  • Preventative Checks: During the "Save" action in SAP, a custom check against vectorized data can alert the user to a potential duplicate.

  • External Access: Quick searches via Microsoft Teams or SAP Fiori to find existing masters on the go.

  • Smart Procurement: Identifying an existing Material Master during the ordering process, preventing the user from resorting to a "text item."

 Pic.2 Quick Searches of SAP Master Data based on Vectorized Data

Conclusion

Generative AI is a powerhouse when used wisely. Many companies struggle to find the right use cases, often just mirroring existing SAP processes in a chat window. That isn’t true AI automation.

Our approach is different: we focus on the gaps in standard automation. By using AI to simplify data discovery, we reduce development effort and provide an intelligent, frictionless experience for SAP users.

Closing Statement

Keep automating. Remember: smart customization is a competitive advantage, not a deviation from the standard. Build with AI in mind to slash support costs and maximize your ROI via the Skybuffer AI platform.

entry.title: Integration Suite in 2025 & 2026
entry.content:
Watched this beautiful session by @UdoPaltzer: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kFynsigf99o

In case you wanted a quick summary, here you go:

Modernize Integrations:

  • Use standard content as much as you can
  • Try to replace your RFCs and IDOCs with OData, Rest, and SOAP.
  • Leverage Event-driven Integration pattern (for real-time scenarios)
  • Use Side-by-side extensions to leverage API-centric integrations (with Build)
  • Leverage monitoring and error resolution capabilities by using SAP AIF (for Business Users), and Cloud ALM (for technical users)

 

End of maintenance. Time is ticking!

  • 2027 - SAP PO | Standard Maintenance sunset
  • 2030 - SAP PO | Extended maintenance sunset
  • 2028 - Cloud Platform Integration Neo | Services sunset

As far as your migration support is concerned, SAP provides Migration guides, documentation, and community content, which you are already aware of. What is additionally provided is:

  • SAP Migration Factory | a free 2-3 weeks program to help you assess and plan your migration from PI/PO to Integration Suite
  • Evolve Neo Program | free access to SAP experts who will help you plan and prepare your migration to Cloud Foundry
  • SAP Service and Support | quick migration assistance with short duration, low cost engagements

Edge Integration Cell 

  • The latest kid in the block is now flexible to run hybrid integration runtime, which is offered as an optional extension to Integration Suite, which will help customer-managed private landscape to leverage the capabilities of Integration Suite. 
  • The landscapes it supports are: Microsoft AKS, Amazon EKS, Red Hat OpenShift, SUSE Rancher, Google GKE. 

Cloud ALM

  • Your one stop for end-to-end technical health monitoring for: SAP BTP, S/4HANA, S/4HANA Cloud, SuccessFactors, Integration Suite, Customer Experience, Business Network, Concur, Fieldglass.
  • You can configure the Oubound Channels like: email alerts, on chat-based apps like MS Teams/ Slack, Ticketing System like Service Now, Tasks like JIRA, APIs like SAP Analytics Cloud/ Grafana, and Operation Automations like  Build Process Automation, and SAP iRPA, etc.
  • For us Integration Suite guys, you can use it for Cloud Integratio, API Management and Event Mesh as well.
  • Specifically for Integration Suite, you can now monitor Tenant Availability, Certificate Validity, Database and Resource consumption, Content, Exhaustion status of JMS queue. So, no more building custom iflows to trigger alerts. Please know that CALM pulls data every 5 mins, and persists data for 30 days. I wonder if they have a feature to display the trace only for failed messages. SAP, are you listening?

Innovations in 2025

  • Artificial Intelligence
    - Joule copilot-based discovery and reuse of artifacts were made available in Business Accelerator Hub
    - Generation of Integration flow steps 
    - Groovy script optimization
    - API anomaly detection
    - API traffic prediction for usage and trend
    - AI adapter
  • Business Accelerators
    - Additional non-SAP adaptors like, AI, Google, BigQuery, IBM MQ (JMS), BigQuery, Microsoft Azure Service Bus, Microsoft OneNote, Mongo DB, Salesforce Pub/ Sub, Shopify, etc
    - New design guidelines to ensure upgrade readiness of Groovy Scripts (Camel 3 to Camel 4)
  • Migration & Modernization
    - Generate ABAP proxy in the backend without any dependency on PI
    - Edge Integration Cell: Local OData API access, HANA DB, Shared Kubernetes, Basis Authentication during offline mode 
    - Alerting of failed B2B messages in CALM
    - Automatic B2B interface creation based on Payload
  • API-centric & Event-driven integration
    - Lightweight adapter in the context of Advanced Event Mesh for small, light-weight, event-driven integration with non-SAP applications
    - Migration support from EM to AEM

Agent-ready Application: IPaaS & AI Market Evolution & Trends

  • Agent iPaaS
  • Agent Integrations
  • Agent Orchestration
  • AI TRiSm (trusted relationship governance of the AI agents

SAP Integration Suite to become the trusted AI Integration Fabric: IS is evolving to become a trusted AI Integration fabric

Some of the use cases are:

  • Integration for AI (to make agentic interactions & orchestration to make applications agent-ready)
    • MCP Gateway
    • Agent Orchestration
    • AI Gateway
    • Agent Identity Verification
    • LLM connector
  • AI for Integration (features in IS)
    • Joule
    • Anomaly detection
    • API traffic predictions
    • Script Optimization
    • Configuration Agent

 

2026 SAP Integration Suite Strategic Outlook:

  • Leveraging Joule for building Integration flows
  • MCP enablement for APIs
  • Migration Agents for Java Mappings & adapter modules
  • AEM events in developer hub
  • 15+ new third-party adapters including Adobe Sign, Google Suite, Oracle, OFTP2, Salesforce Service & Marketing, Cloud, Oracle Eloqua
  • Joule Skills & AI Agents in SAP Business Accelerator Hub
  • B2B capabilities in Edge Integration Cell

Roadmap for SAP Integration Suite:

  • Artificial Intelligence
    - Integration flow design validation
    - Joule based generation of iflows
    - MCP for agentic AI
    - Migration agent for adapter modules, Java mapping
    - Configuration Agent
    - Payload-size anomaly detection
  • Business Accelerators
    - More connectivity options, meaning, more adapters like Google Drive & Sheets Microsoft Excel & Outlook, OFTP2, RosettaNet, Salesforce Service Cloud, etc
    - Support for publications of Joule Skills & Agents in Business Accelerator Hub
    -     Commercial module for Partner Content. Consumers/Customers with a licence to Partner Content can now use it. Currently, customers can copy and use it for free, but from now on, the IP rights are protected, so you can use it only if you have a licence.
  • Migration & Modernization
    -     Mass migration from PO to IS
    -     Edge Integration Cell: customer-managed cloud connectors, offline mode support for 48 hours, B2B capabilities, multiple instances of EIC per Kubernetes Cluster
    -     SAP IS data centers in Australia, Germany, India, Japan, US.
  • API-Centric & Event-driven integrations
    -     AEM health monitoring in SAP Cloud ALM
    -     Auto discovery and publications of events from AEM to Developer Hub as single catalog for APIs and Events
    -     Consumption of events through Developer Hub

entry.title: Joule integration with SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite – Setup Guide
entry.content:
Introduction:

While SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite helps organizations enhance operational efficiency, drive innovation, and achieve strategic business objectives, we are happy to introduce SAP Copilot–Joule. With Joule integration, you will be able to interact with your system using Joule patterns such as Informational, Transactional, and Navigational scenarios, and leverage multiple capabilities from SAP Signavio. You will be able to interact with:

  • SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub
  • SAP Signavio Journey Modeler
  • SAP Signavio Process Intelligence
  • Value accelerator library for SAP Signavio solutions

Before we get started with the Joule integration with SAP Signavio, it’s important to understand the overall Joule integration for your organization. Please look at the blogs below:

This blog post is a series for Joule Activation:

  • SAP Business AI – Overview for all !!! (Includes information on Joule Supported Data Centers, Different scenarios during Joule setup, etc..)
  • Joule - Unified Setup: Bridging Simplicity and Performance (Demo Video to understand the Unified Setup, Joule basics & architecture, how to set up Joule, etc...) 
  • Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide
  • Joule Setup – End-to-End Setup Guide (for all Line of Business) Unified Approach 
  • Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Setup Guide
  • SAP Digital Supply Chain
    • Joule for SAP Integrated Business Planning (SAP IBP) – Setup Guide
    • Joule Setup for SAP Integrated Product Development
  • Joule – Getting Started with Document Grounding - setup guide
  • Activate Joule with SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Mobile Start
  • Analytical Insights
    • Analytical Insights in Joule – Setup Guide
  • Joule Integration with SAP Ariba - Setup Guide
  • Joule Integration with SAP Concur - Setup Guide

👉SAP Signavio - you are here

Blogs - Work in Progress (WIP):

  • SAP Digital Supply Chain (WIP)
    • SAP Asset Performance Management
    • SAP Product Lifecycle Management
    • SAP Digital Manufacturing
  • and more to be added towards General Availability

****************************************************************************

It is always recommended to check the SAP Official Help Page for Joule Integration with Signavio Solutions and check the Joule Capabilities (user interactions with Signavio).  

To begin with, let us take a look at the prerequisites for Joule integration with SAP Signavio:

  • You have your SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite
  • Joule Entitlements in your SAP Business Technology Platform - In case you do not have the Joule license you can opt for the 0$ order from SAP Store also be familiar with Joule Base and Premium capabilities 
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services
  • SAP Build Work Zone (planned for next release,  not required for now)

While we plan your Joule integration, it’s important to know your landscape and how you would like to activate Joule.

Image 1

We will explain both the processes, with Section 1 focusing on first-time setup and Section 2 focusing on Joule with an existing setup, to make it easy for our customers. For both these sections, SAP Cloud Identity Service setup is common with your SAP Signavio systems.  

Pre-requisites for Joule activation with SAP Signavio:

  • SAP Signavio
  • SAP Business Technology Platform with Joule Entitlements
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services is working with your SAP Signavio system

 

1. SAP Signavio with SAP Cloud Identity Services

To activate Joule in SAP Signavio, we first need to ensure we have the prerequisites. Let's look at the details of configuring SAP Cloud Identity Services for your system.

Note: In case you already have SAP Cloud Identity configured with SAP Signavio, you may skip this section.

Image 2

To achieve the above setup, you will need access as an Administrator to your SAP Cloud Identity Services and your SAP Signavio system.

We highly recommend checking the Single Sign-on Using SAML help page and the following sections

- Set Up SSO Using SAML

- Configure Your IdP

- Enabling SSO Using SAML

- Invite New Users by Email

- Enforce SSO to Disable Login with Credentials

The details below can guide one of the processes to Setup SSO with SAP Cloud Identity Services.

 

1.1 Download the Metadata file from SAP Cloud Identity Services

This file is required to exchange the SAML 2.0 files and establish trust between the systems. More information is available here. 

 

⚠️Ensure you have selected the same SAP Cloud Identity used across the landscape for SSO. This is essential for the best user experience with Joule. For more details, please visit our previous blog (Joule—Unified Setup: Bridging Simplicity and Performance) and ensure you use the same Domain Name as in your SAP Cloud Identity Services. Note that the Cloud Identity Service tenant cannot vary in case you want to connect multiple solutions to Joule.

Log in to your SAP Cloud Identity Services tenant, click on Applications and Resources, select Tenant Settings -> select Single Sign-On -> click on SAML 2.0 Configurations, and click on Download Metadata File.

Image 3

We shall go ahead and import this metadata file into you SAP Signavio System.

 

1.2 Establish Trust and Download the Metadata file from SAP Signavio

Log in to your SAP Signavio system, click on the App Menu icon, and click on Process Manager.

Image 4

In the Process Manager page, click on Setup and click on Manage SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub authentication as shown below.

Image 5

In the new window, you will have a few options to activate Single Sign-on Using SAML. You may refer to the section Enable SSO Using SAML. You will need to select the following options:

  • Enable SAML 2.0 authentication
  • Allow service provider-initiated authentication
  • Sign authentication request
  • Create new user accounts automatically (this will be an optional parameter, please validate before you select it)
  • Copy the code from your SAP Cloud Identity Services metadata file and paste it into the Metadata section, as shown below

Click on Download the SAML service provider metadata, now this Metadata will be added to the SAP Cloud Identity Services

Image 6

Once you have validated the details, Save it.

1.3 Create an Application for Signavio in SAP Cloud Identity Services

We will be creating a new Application in your SAP Cloud Identity Services to setup a SAML trust between the systems. You may also refer to the official SAP Note - 3461963 for more details.

In your SAP Cloud Identity Services, click on Application & Resources -> click on Applications -> then click on Create.

 

Image 7

In the Create Application, please enter the details to recognize your system. You may follow the details as shown below and Save the settings.

  • Display Name – Signavio System
  • Type: SAP Signavio Solution
  • Protocol Type: SAML 2.0

Image 8

A new application will be created. Please click on the new application you created for Signavio System, click on SAML 2.0 Configuration, click on Define from Metadata, click on Browse in the new window, and upload it.

Image 9

You should be able to see the Metadata file information once it’s uploaded as shown below.

Image 10

In the same section of your application Signavio System, click on Subject Name Identifier change the Identity Directory - Value to Global User ID, and Save the settings.

 

Image 11

Next, we will need to add a value in the Attributes section. Please click on Attributes and click on Add and ensure you add the attribute Name as “signavio_licenses_v1” and the Value in my case I have “Enterprise Plus Edition”  (You can choose your license type - The name of the license that you want to assign to a user, for example "Enterprise Plus Edition", "Enterprise Edition", "Classic Edition", "Collaboration Hub", or "Workflow". If required you can also refer to the note3461963 - Configuring SSO with SAP IAS for SAP Signavio).

Before you save the details, please validate the other details such as email, first_name, last_name, and user_uuid as per the screen, and Save it. 

 

Image 12

In case you want to add more values please look at Configure your IdP section on the help page.

This completes the bidirectional trust between the SAP Signavio system and the SAP Cloud Identity Services.

 

1.4 Test your SAP Signavio SSO with SAP Cloud Identity Services

To access your SAP Signavio system with SSO you can use the following URL syntax:

https://<<ServerRegion>>.signavio.com/p/hub?t=<WORKSPACE_ID> 

In case you do not know your WORKSPACE_ID, within your SAP Signavio system click on the question icon and you can find the Workspace ID as shown below.

 

Image 13

So, in my case, my SAP Signavio SSO URL is:

https://editor.signavio.com/p/hub?t=5xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxc

Once I launch this URL, SAP Signavio auto-redirects to the SAP Cloud Identity Services page for Authentication, and users should be able to log in successfully.

Further, you can check the roles and authorization management here - Manage Users and Access Rights.

 

1.5 SAP Cloud Identity Services Additional Configurations
